differentials.raw: sensorineural / mixed hearing loss (cochlear involvement / stapes fixation in oval window)

differentials.name: Fibrous dysplasia monostotic with temporal bone involvement Cause:   inability to form mature lamellar bone owing to disordered osteoblastic activity

differentials.raw: conductive hearing loss ← narrowing of EAC / middle ear

differentials.name: Otosyphilis: labyrinthitis + gummatous lesion of internal auditory canal + inflammatory
differentials.raw: Otosyphilis: labyrinthitis + gummatous lesion of internal auditory canal + inflammatory

context: √  usually lytic changes beginning in petrous pyramid + progressing laterally; otic capsule last to be affected √  calvarial changes ± basilar impression √  homogeneously dense thickened structurally weak bone (fibro-osseous tissue less dense than calvarial bone) √  bone expansion with preserved cortex → osseous narrowing of vascular channels + neuroforamina √  lytic lesions (less frequent) √  sparing of membranous labyrinth, facial nerve canal, IAC is the rule √  changes similar to otosclerosis van der Hoeve-de Kleyn syndrome =  osteogenesis imperfecta + hearing loss + blue sclerae in patient
